metadata.title: Legacy Biomarker Qualification Project Status Update: Glutamate Dehydrogenase (GLDH) as Safety Biomarker for Drug-Induced Liver Injury

rules.rule_text: GLDH activity can be used as a safety biomarker for monitoring specifically for hepatocellular injury in healthy subjects and patients in conjunction with a panel of standard DILI biomarkers in all stages of drug development trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Serum enzymatic activity of glutamate dehydrogenase (GLDH) is a safety biomarker for monitoring specifically for hepatocellular injury in healthy subjects and patients in conjunction with a panel of standard DILI biomarkers in all stages of drug development trials.
cross_references: FDA Guidance for Industry Drug-Induced Liver Injury: Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009)

rules.rule_text: A change of 2.5x and 5x upper limit of normal (ULN) for GLDH activity corresponds to 3x and 5x ULN ALT and these fold changes can be utilized along with the standard hepatic injury monitoring panel for DILI assessment.
rules.verbatim_quote: A change of 2.5x and 5x upper limit of normal (ULN) for glutamate dehydrogenase (GLDH) activity levels corresponds to 3x and 5x ULN ALT. These fold changes of GLDH can be utilized along with the standard hepatic injury monitoring panel for the assessment of drug-induced liver injury (DILI), in the same manner as 3x and 5x ULN ALT.
rules.exceptions: Although absolute values of ULN for GLDH will vary from region to region, the 2.5x and 5x fold change from ULN should not change.
cross_references: FDA Guidance for Industry Drug-Induced Liver Injury: Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009)

rules.rule_text: If GLDH activity increases to 2.5x ULN or 5x ULN, the same precautions should be taken as if 3x or 5x ULN ALT increases are observed as described in the FDA Guidance for Industry Drug-Induced Liver Injury: Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009).
rules.verbatim_quote: If GLDH activity increases to 2.5x ULN or 5x ULN, the same precautions should be taken as if 3x or 5x ULN ALT increases are observed as described in the FDA Guidance for Industry Drug-Induced Liver Injury: Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009).
cross_references: FDA Guidance for Industry Drug-Induced Liver Injury: Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009)

rules.rule_text: If GLDH is not elevated in subjects with unexplained ALT elevations, liver can be ruled out as a target tissue of toxicity and diagnosis of alternative sources of ALT increases must be sought via biomarkers of muscle injury such as creatinine kinase activity.
rules.verbatim_quote: If GLDH is not elevated in the previously defined subjects, liver can be ruled out as a target tissue of toxicity and diagnosis of alternative sources of ALT increases should be sought, (i.e., via biomarkers of muscle injury such as creatinine kinase activity).
cross_references: FDA Guidance for Industry Drug-Induced Liver Injury: Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009)

rules.rule_text: GLDH is intended for use in First-in-Human and other Phase I Single Ascending Dose or Multiple Ascending Doses studies, as well as Phase II and III studies in patients.
rules.source_section: Section I.B (Stage of Drug Development for Use)
rules.verbatim_quote: Stage of Drug Development for Use: First-in-Human and other Phase I Single Ascending Dose or Multiple Ascending Doses studies, as well as Phase II and III studies in patients.

rules.rule_text: GLDH is intended for use in normal healthy volunteers and patients with increases in alanine aminotransferase (ALT) from suspected extrahepatic sources such as muscle.
rules.verbatim_quote: Target Population for Use: Normal healthy volunteers and patients with increases in alanine aminotransferase (ALT) from suspected extrahepatic sources such as muscle.

rules.rule_text: GLDH activity must be measured in serum and not in other biological matrices.
rules.verbatim_quote: Matrix: serum

rules.rule_text: The reference range for GLDH in human serum is less than 3 to 10 U/L on the Siemens Advia 1800 Chemistry Analyzer platform.
rules.verbatim_quote: the reference range interval for GLDH in human serum was updated to <3 to 10 U/L.
rules.exceptions: Absolute values of ULN for GLDH will vary from region to region.

rules.rule_text: The GLDH assay must be performed in a Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA)-certified laboratory when used as a qualified biomarker.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the biomarker is qualified, will the test/assay be performed in a Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA)–certified laboratory? Yes

rules.rule_text: A standard operating procedure (SOP) for sample collection and storage must exist for the GLDH assay.
rules.verbatim_quote: Is there a standard operating procedure (SOP) for sample collection and storage? Yes

rules.rule_text: A laboratory SOP for the test/assay methodology must exist for the GLDH assay.
rules.verbatim_quote: Is there a laboratory SOP for the test/assay methodology? Yes

rules.rule_text: GLDH assay precision must demonstrate a coefficient of variation (CV) less than 10% for quality control material and human serum samples, with CVs up to 15% acceptable for samples near the detection level.
rules.verbatim_quote: The GLDH human serum assay demonstrated appropriate precision, as per the analyzed human serum samples and quality control material (<10% coefficient of variation (CV)).
rules.exceptions: Higher CV values at low concentrations (near LOD) are acceptable since CV is impacted more substantially by changes in small values.

rules.rule_text: The GLDH assay recovery must fall within 80-120% at all concentrations across the assay's dynamic range.
rules.verbatim_quote: The GLDH human serum assay demonstrated acceptable recovery. All samples had appropriate percent recovery values (80 - 120%) based upon the results of this testing and the acceptance criteria.

rules.rule_text: GLDH samples must demonstrate acceptable stability: room temperature up to 48 hours, refrigerated up to 14 days, and frozen at -80°C up to 18 months.
rules.verbatim_quote: GLDH in human serum demonstrates acceptable stability at room temperature up to 48 hours, refrigerated up to 14 days, and frozen at -80°C up to 18 months.

rules.rule_text: GLDH samples must demonstrate acceptable freeze-thaw stability for at least 4 freeze-thaw cycles with 80-120% recovery of initial thaw concentration.
rules.source_section: Section V.C (Biomarker Measurement - Sample Freeze/Thaw Stability)
rules.verbatim_quote: GLDH in human serum demonstrates acceptable stability for 4 freeze thaw cycles.

rules.rule_text: High concentrations of triglycerides cause interference with GLDH testing; samples positive for high triglycerides must be omitted from analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: All samples met the acceptance criteria with the exception of lipemia. At high concentrations of triglycerides there is interference with GLDH testing... Any sample positive for high triglycerides was omitted from the analysis and had no impact on the results.
rules.exceptions: Hemolysis (hemoglobin) and icterus (bilirubin) do not interfere with the measurement of GLDH.

rules.rule_text: When using GLDH in place of ALT, the EWG definition of hepatotoxicity must be applied: hepatotoxicity is met if one of the following is true: greater than or equal to 2.5 times ULN for GLDH and greater than or equal to 2 times ULN for total bilirubin; or greater than or equal to 5 times ULN for GLDH; or greater than or equal to 2 times ULN for alkaline phosphatase.
rules.source_section: Section VI.B (Anticipated Risks and Risk Mitigation Strategy)
rules.verbatim_quote: When GLDH is used, in place of ALT, the EWG definition of hepatotoxicity is met if one of the following statements is true: ≥2.5 times the upper limit of normal (ULN) for GLDH and ≥2 times the ULN for Tbil, OR ≥5 times the ULN for GLDH, OR ≥2 times the ULN for ALP.
cross_references: FDA Guidance for Industry Drug-Induced Liver Injury: Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009)

rules.rule_text: GLDH must be used as a complement to and not as a replacement for the existing standard methods for assessing potential DILI in clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: GLDH activity is proposed to be utilized as a complement to the existing guidance and standard methods for assessing DILI.
cross_references: FDA Guidance for Industry Drug-Induced Liver Injury: Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009)

rules.rule_text: Assay linearity for GLDH must be established from 3 to 500 U/L on the Siemens Advia 1800 Chemistry Analyzer platform.
rules.verbatim_quote: the current assay on the Siemens Advia 1800 Chemistry Analyzer has an established assay linearity of 3 to 500 U/L, thereby resulting in a reference range for GLDH in human serum of <3 to 10 U/L.

rules.rule_text: The GLDH qualification study to confirm GLDH as a biomarker of liver injury in humans must demonstrate that sensitivity, specificity, and concordance measures each achieve a target success criterion of greater than or equal to 0.90 with a 95% lower confidence bound greater than or equal to 0.85.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.B (Knowledge Gaps - Statistical Analysis Plan)
rules.verbatim_quote: The target success for each measure is ≥ 0.90, 95% Lower Confidence Bound ≥ 0.85.

rules.rule_text: For confirmation that GLDH is unaffected by muscle injury, the false positive rate (FPR) target success criteria are: at ULN threshold, FPR must be less than or equal to 10%; at 2.5x ULN threshold, FPR must be less than or equal to 5%; at 5x ULN threshold, FPR must be less than or equal to 1%.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.B (Knowledge Gaps - Statistical Analysis Plan)
rules.verbatim_quote: the percentage of subjects with GLDH values exceeding ULN (10), 2.5X ULN (25) and 5X ULN (50) will be computed. The target success criteria are: ULN: FPR ≤ 10%; 2.5X ULN: FPR ≤ 5%; 5X ULN: FPR ≤ 1%

rules.rule_text: GLDH specificity must be confirmed by demonstrating that GLDH is unaffected by pancreatic, gastrointestinal, and kidney injuries in humans.
rules.verbatim_quote: Confirmation that serum glutamate dehydrogenase (GLDH) is unaffected by pancreatic, gastrointestinal and kidney injuries in humans

rules.rule_text: For confirmatory studies on GLDH specificity for pancreatic/gastrointestinal/kidney injury, the FPR target success criteria at 2.5x ULN must be less than or equal to 5% and at 5x ULN must be less than or equal to 1%.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.B (Knowledge Gaps - Statistical Analysis Plan)
rules.verbatim_quote: the percentage of subjects with GLDH values exceeding 2.5X ULN (25) and 5X ULN (50) will be computed. The target success criteria are: 2.5X ULN: FPR ≤ 5%; 5.X ULN: FPR ≤ 1%

rules.rule_text: For confirmation that GLDH detects the onset of liver injury by acetaminophen, all subjects must have GLDH levels greater than ULN at time of hospital admission.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.B (Knowledge Gaps - Statistical Analysis Plan)
rules.verbatim_quote: Confirmation that serum glutamate dehydrogenase (GLDH) detects the onset of liver injury by acetaminophen will have the following primary analysis... Target success criteria: all subjects have GLDH greater than ULN.

rules.rule_text: The GLDH assay validation must include establishment of the following parameters: limit of blank (LOB), limit of detection (LOD), precision, method-to-method comparison, linearity, accuracy/recovery, reference interval, freeze/thaw stability, sample stability, and analytical specificity/interference.
rules.verbatim_quote: Validation parameters for GLDH on the Advia 1800 platform are shown below. Table 1 shows assay validation parameters, testing strategy, and acceptance criteria.
